metadata.label: extra/HealthEquity-DotNet
metadata.title: Software Engineer II
metadata.team_direction: HealthEquity is dedicated to saving and improving lives by empowering healthcare consumers

official_notes: No recovered historical Claude-vs-official target baseline; used only for old-pipe vs new-pipe Codex comparison.

revision_priority: Compress the Skills section so each category stays within the 4-8 item density rule.
Remove bolding from qualifier words like `team-maintained` and add a clearer healthcare-domain bridge in the summary.

findings.field: Summary sentence 1; Summary sentence 2; TikTok Experience bullet 2; TikTok Experience bullet 5
findings.issue: Several scan-critical quantitative and technical tokens are left unbolded, including `3+ years`, `Agile`, and `Bedrock`.
findings.fix: Bold those evidence-bearing tokens in place wherever they appear, without changing the wording.

findings.field: TikTok Experience bullets 1-4; DiDi Experience bullets 1-4
findings.issue: Ownership and scope qualifiers that matter for credibility are plain text, such as `team-maintained`, `internal`, `operator-facing`, and `shared`.
findings.fix: Bold the qualifiers that define ownership, boundary, or responsibility when they carry signal value.

findings.issue: The opening frame leads with `trajectory` and `transitioning from data analytics` instead of a direct role-and-domain anchor, so the first impression is career shift rather than target fit.
findings.fix: Rewrite the header to a direct fit statement such as `Security-focused Backend Software Engineer:` or `Backend Engineer:` and move the transition story later.

revision_priority: [MUST] Reframe Summary sentence 1 to lead with a direct backend/security role statement instead of a transition narrative.
[MUST] Bold the missing high-signal tokens in the body and Summary (`3+ years`, `Agile`, `Bedrock`, `team-maintained`, `internal`, `operator-facing`).
[SHOULD] Keep the rest of the content unchanged; do not remove any technical evidence that supports JD fit.
